item.title: Autonomous Quadcopter
item.description:
This project was undertaken by the Team Robocon. It is a fully funded by the BITS Alumni Association (BITSAA). We aim to build quadcopter achieving a level 5 autonomy.

I am leading the State Estimation subsystem of the project. Currently, the plan is to fuse the RTAB-Map and the Extended Kalman Filter algorithms to accomplish the goal.

item.title: GenNav
item.description:
GenNav is a python library for robotics navigation algorithms and utilities that are commonly required in navigation stacks. The library aims to be completely modular and have a unified API so that it is usable in a broad range of applications and easily extensible to new robotics domains.

As a part of the team, I implemented path planning algorithms like Rapidly‐exploring Random Tree (RRT), Rapidly‐exploring Random Graph (RRG), some utility functions, etc. for a general robot navigation package. I also made Jupyter and Google Collab notebook tutorials for showing how to use the available algorithms.

item.title: Path Planner and Autonomous Controller for a Robot
item.description: Created an autonomous PID controller and a Path Planner using Randomly Exploring Random Tree (RRT) for a Trotbot robot. Trotbot is created by the Electronics and Robotics Club at BITS Goa. It is based on an Omnibase package developed by them. The Trotbot planned it’s way through a series of static obstacles, and traversed the resultant path using the PID controller code I wrote. I have used ROS and Python for completing the project. I simulated my code on Gazebo.
